attrs.alt: PIM just-in-time access lifecycle: eligible → activate → approve → active → auto-expire

content.text: What PIM Solves

Privileged Identity Management (PIM) is an Entra ID Governance service that eliminates standing privileged access. Standing access means a user holds an active role assignment 24/7 regardless of whether they are using it. PIM replaces standing access with just-in-time (JIT) access: users hold an 'eligible' assignment that only becomes active when they explicitly request it and prove need.

Standing access risk: if an account with Owner access is compromised, the attacker immediately has full control. With PIM, the account is eligible for Owner but not active — the attacker would also need to pass MFA and meet any approval requirements to activate.

Audit trail: every PIM activation is a recorded event with the user identity, role, scope, duration, justification, and optionally a ticket number. Standing assignments have no equivalent activation record.
Expiry enforcement: PIM roles expire automatically at the end of the configured duration. No cleanup required — the role reverts to eligible on its own.
Access reviews: PIM integrates with Entra ID Governance access reviews to periodically certify that users should still hold eligible assignments.

PIM is not just a security feature — it is an audit and compliance tool. Demonstrating JIT access with enforced MFA and justification satisfies privileged access requirements in SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, and most enterprise security frameworks.
PIM Scope: Azure Resources and Entra Roles
PIM manages two distinct types of roles through separate sub-services. Both require Entra ID P2 or Microsoft Entra ID Governance licensing.
PIM Scope
Covers
Managed via
PowerShell/API
Azure resources
Azure RBAC roles (Owner, Contributor, custom roles) at MG/Sub/RG/Resource scope
Entra ID portal → PIM → Azure resources
Azure Resource Manager REST API (roleEligibilityScheduleRequests)
Microsoft Entra roles
Entra directory roles (Global Admin, User Admin, etc.)
Entra ID portal → PIM → Microsoft Entra roles
Microsoft Graph API (roleManagement/directory/roleEligibilityScheduleRequests)
Groups (preview)
Group membership as a proxy for both role types
Entra ID portal → PIM → Groups
MS Graph (identityGovernance/privilegedAccess/group)
PIM for Groups is a powerful pattern: make a user eligible for a group, and the group holds both Azure RBAC roles and Entra roles. One PIM activation grants access to both systems simultaneously.
Assignment Types
PIM introduces a distinction between eligible and active assignments that does not exist in standard role management.
Assignment Type
Access State
How created
When to use
Eligible
No access until activated
PIM assignment by privileged role admin
Most privileged roles — default choice for JIT
Active (time-bounded)
Immediate access, expires at set date
PIM assignment with active state
Break-glass accounts; contractor access with firm end date
Active (permanent)
Immediate access, no expiry
Standard role assignment OR PIM with 'Allow permanent active'
Emergency access accounts; automation service principals that cannot interactively authenticate
Enabling PIM and Prerequisites

PIM is available to all tenants with Entra ID P2 or Microsoft Entra ID Governance licensing. No explicit 'activation' step is required — the service is available once licensed. You configure it by navigating to Privileged Identity Management in the Entra ID portal or by using the Microsoft Graph API.

# PIM is enabled per-role/per-scope through the Entra ID portal or Microsoft Graph.
# Prerequisite: Entra ID P2 or Microsoft Entra ID Governance license.

# Check whether PIM is available in the tenant
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'RoleManagement.Read.Directory'
(Get-MgSubscribedSku | Where-Object { $_.ServicePlans.ServicePlanName -contains 'AAD_PREMIUM_P2' }).SkuPartNumber
# Should return 'AAD_PREMIUM_P2' or 'ENTERPRISEPREMIUM' if P2 is licensed.

# Configure PIM settings for an Azure resource role (via az rest)
# PIM policy defines: max activation duration, MFA requirement, approval requirement
$scope = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
$token = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token

# List existing PIM role management policies at a scope
$uri = "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleManagementPolicies?api-version=2020-10-01"
Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET -Uri $uri -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value | Select-Object name, @{n='Scope';e={$_.properties.scope}}
Required license: Entra ID P2 (included in Microsoft 365 E5, EMS E5) or Microsoft Entra ID Governance add-on.
License must be assigned to each user who will hold PIM-eligible assignments or who approves PIM requests.
Privileged Role Administrator or Global Administrator role required to configure PIM settings and create eligible assignments.
PIM for Azure resources also requires the User Access Administrator or Owner role at the scope being managed.
Creating Eligible Assignments
An eligible assignment designates a user (or group) as authorized to activate a specific role. The assignment does not grant any permissions until the user activates it.

# Create a PIM eligible assignment for an Azure resource role
$principalId  = (Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName 'alice@contoso.com').Id
$roleDefId    = (Get-AzRoleDefinition -Name 'Contributor').Id
$scope        = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
$token        = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token

# Create an eligible schedule request (not an active role assignment)
$body = @{
    properties = @{
        principalId      = $principalId
        roleDefinitionId = "/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/$roleDefId"
        requestType      = 'AdminAssign'
        scheduleInfo     = @{
            startDateTime = (Get-Date -Format 'o')
            expiration    = @{ type = 'AfterDuration'; duration = 'P180D' }  # 180 days eligible
        }
        justification    = 'Quarterly access grant for project Blue'
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10

$uri = "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleRequests/$([System.Guid]::NewGuid())?api-version=2020-10-01"
Invoke-RestMethod -Method PUT -Uri $uri -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token"; 'Content-Type' = 'application/json' } -Body $body

# The user can now activate this role in the Azure portal under
# PIM → My roles → Azure resource roles → Eligible assignments.
Eligible assignments have an optional expiry date. After expiry, the user can no longer activate the role until a new eligible assignment is created.
PIM recommends setting a maximum eligible assignment duration (e.g., 180 or 365 days) to force periodic renewal and review.
Eligible assignments appear under 'My roles' in the Entra ID portal, not in the standard IAM role assignments blade.
Get-AzRoleAssignment does NOT show eligible assignments — they are invisible to standard Azure RBAC tooling. You must query the roleEligibilityScheduleInstances API.
Activating a Role

Users activate eligible roles through the Entra ID portal (recommended for interactive use) or via the REST API (for scripted scenarios). The activation creates a time-bounded active role assignment that expires automatically.

# A user activating their own PIM eligible role via PowerShell
# (normally done via the Entra ID portal, but scriptable via REST API)
$scope    = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
$roleDefId = (Get-AzRoleDefinition -Name 'Contributor').Id
$token     = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token
$myId      = (Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName (Get-AzContext).Account.Id).Id

# Get the eligible schedule instance ID (needed to reference the eligible assignment)
$eligible = Invoke-RestMethod `
    -Method GET `
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleInstances?api-version=2020-10-01&`$filter=principalId eq '$myId'" `
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" }
$scheduleId = $eligible.value[0].name

# Submit a self-activation request
$activateBody = @{
    properties = @{
        principalId                   = $myId
        roleDefinitionId              = "/subscriptions/.../roleDefinitions/$roleDefId"
        requestType                   = 'SelfActivate'
        linkedRoleEligibilityScheduleId = $scheduleId
        scheduleInfo = @{
            startDateTime = (Get-Date -Format 'o')
            expiration    = @{ type = 'AfterDuration'; duration = 'PT2H' }  # 2 hours active
        }
        justification = 'Deploying hotfix for incident INC-4892'
        ticketInfo    = @{ ticketNumber = 'INC-4892'; ticketSystem = 'ServiceNow' }
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10

Invoke-RestMethod -Method PUT `
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignmentScheduleRequests/$([System.Guid]::NewGuid())?api-version=2020-10-01" `
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token"; 'Content-Type' = 'application/json' } `
    -Body $activateBody
User navigates to PIM → My roles → Azure resource roles → Eligible assignments and clicks Activate.
User selects the activation duration (up to the policy maximum) and enters a justification. If ticket information is required, they provide the ticket number.
If approval is required, the request enters a pending state and approvers are notified. Access is not granted until approval.
Once approved (or immediately if no approval required), a time-bounded active role assignment is created.
The user's current session token does not change — they must obtain a new token (re-authenticate) for the new role to appear in the token.

After PIM activation, users must refresh their session to get a new token that includes the newly active role. In the Azure portal, signing out and back in is usually sufficient. For CLI/SDK sessions, run az logout && az login or Disconnect-AzAccount && Connect-AzAccount.
PIM Policy Configuration

PIM policies define the rules for each role at each scope. The policy for 'Contributor at subscription X' can differ from 'Contributor at subscription Y'. Policies are configured per role per scope in the Entra ID portal or via the roleManagementPolicy REST API.

# PIM policies control the settings for each role at each scope.
# These are configured in the Entra ID portal under PIM → Azure resources
# → [subscription] → Settings → [role name].
# Key settings per role:

# 1. Maximum activation duration (default: 8 hours, max configurable: 24 hours)
#    Shorter durations = less standing access; must balance with operational friction.

# 2. Activation requirements (for sensitive roles, require ALL of these):
#    - Require MFA on activation: user must complete MFA even if already authenticated
#    - Require justification: user must enter a reason (free text)
#    - Require ticket information: user provides incident/change ticket number
#    - Require approval: a designated approver group must approve before access is granted

# 3. Notification settings:
#    - Alert when eligible assignment created
#    - Alert when role is activated
#    - Alert when active assignment (non-PIM permanent) is created

# 4. Assignment settings:
#    - Allow permanent eligible assignment (vs require expiry date)
#    - Allow permanent active assignment (vs require expiry date)
#    - Maximum eligible assignment duration (e.g., 365 days)

# Update policy via REST (patch the roleManagementPolicy rule for the role):
# This requires reading the current policy rules and patching the specific rule ID.
# Easier to do in the portal for initial setup; use REST for automation/IaC.

# Recommended settings for high-privilege roles (Owner, Contributor at sub scope):
# - Max duration: 4 hours
# - Require MFA: Yes
# - Require justification: Yes
# - Require ticket: Yes (link to ITSM)
# - Require approval: Yes (manager or security team)
# - Alert on every activation: Yes
Access Reviews for PIM Eligibility

Access reviews integrate with PIM to periodically certify that users should retain their eligible assignments. An uncertified user can have their eligibility automatically removed, preventing accumulation of dormant privileged access.

# PIM integrates with Entra ID Governance Access Reviews.
# Access reviews periodically certify who should keep eligible assignments.

Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AccessReview.ReadWrite.All'

# Create an access review for PIM-eligible Contributor assignments
$review = @{
    displayName       = 'Quarterly PIM Contributor Review'
    startDate         = (Get-Date).ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')
    endDate           = (Get-Date).AddDays(14).ToString('yyyy-MM-dd')
    reviewers         = @(@{ query = '/me'; queryType = 'MicrosoftGraph' })  # self-review
    reviewerType      = 'Managers'  # or 'Self', 'Assigned'
    scope             = @{
        '@odata.type'  = '#microsoft.graph.principalResourceMembershipsScope'
        principalScopes = @(@{ '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.accessReviewQueryScope'; query = '/users' })
        resourceScopes  = @(@{ '@odata.type' = '#microsoft.graph.accessReviewQueryScope'; query = '/roleManagement/directory/roleDefinitions/<role-id>' })
    }
    settings = @{
        mailNotificationsEnabled    = $true
        reminderNotificationsEnabled = $true
        applyRecommendations         = $true  # auto-apply based on last sign-in
        autoApplyDecisionsEnabled    = $true
        defaultDecision              = 'Deny'  # deny if reviewer doesn't respond
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10

New-MgIdentityGovernanceAccessReviewDefinition -BodyParameter ($review | ConvertFrom-Json)
Configure access reviews to recur quarterly for most roles; monthly for highly sensitive roles like Global Administrator or Owner at subscription scope.
Set defaultDecision = 'Deny' so that inaction from a reviewer results in access removal rather than approval — explicit certify-forward is safer.
applyRecommendations = true uses Entra's sign-in data to suggest Deny for users who haven't used the role in 30+ days.
Access review decisions are logged and exportable for compliance evidence.
Auditing PIM Activations

Every PIM activation and deactivation is a recorded event in both the Entra audit log (for Entra roles) and the Azure Activity Log (for resource roles). Regular audit review of activations detects anomalies: after-hours activations, unusual scopes, missing justifications.

# Audit PIM activations to detect unusual access patterns
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All','PrivilegedAccess.Read.AzureResources'

# List all PIM activations in the last 24 hours (Entra roles)
$start = (Get-Date).AddHours(-24).ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')
Get-MgAuditLogDirectoryAudit `
    -Filter "activityDisplayName eq 'Add member to role completed (PIM activation)' and activityDateTime ge $start" |
    Select-Object ActivityDateTime, @{n='User';e={$_.InitiatedBy.User.UserPrincipalName}},
                  @{n='Role';e={($_.TargetResources | Where-Object Type -eq 'Role').DisplayName}},
                  Result

# For Azure resource role activations, query via REST
$token = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token
$scope = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'

Invoke-RestMethod `
    -Method GET `
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignmentScheduleRequests?api-version=2020-10-01&`$filter=requestType eq 'SelfActivate'" `
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } | Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object @{n='Principal';e={$_.properties.principalId}},
                  @{n='Role';e={$_.properties.roleDefinitionId.Split('/')[-1]}},
                  @{n='Justification';e={$_.properties.justification}},
                  @{n='CreatedOn';e={$_.properties.createdOn}}
Audit Signal
Investigate When
Source
Activation outside business hours
Role activated at 2 AM on a weekend
Entra audit log / Azure Activity Log
Activation with generic justification
'testing' or 'maintenance' as justification on a production scope
PIM activation event justification field
Rapid successive activations
Same user activates Owner on 10 subscriptions in 5 minutes
Entra audit log — batch by principal + time window
Approval bypass
Active assignment created directly (not through PIM activation) on a role that requires PIM
Azure Activity Log — roleAssignments/write not from PIM
Expired eligible not renewed
User's eligible assignment expired 30 days ago but they still have active access
Cross-reference eligibility schedule vs active schedule
Summary
PIM replaces permanent standing access with just-in-time eligible assignments that only grant permissions when explicitly activated.
PIM covers both Azure RBAC roles (at any ARM scope) and Microsoft Entra directory roles, plus group membership (preview).
Activation requires MFA and justification (configurable), optionally an approval workflow and ticket number.
Every activation creates a time-bounded active assignment that expires automatically — no manual cleanup.
PIM policies are per-role-per-scope: configure strict settings (short duration, approval required) for the most sensitive roles.
Integrate PIM with access reviews to periodically certify eligible assignments and automatically remove unused eligibility.
Audit every activation — unusual patterns (after-hours, missing justification, mass activations) warrant immediate investigation.
